headline: What Applied Materials Stock Was Shouting Before The Surge
summary: Before the stock took off, the company's overall growth looked sluggish, but management was explicitly forecasting a boom in its most important AI-related businesses.

headline: Applied Materials (AMAT) Climbed Amid Broad-Based Growth Drivers
summary: Renaissance Investment Management, an investment management company, released its Q1 2026 “Large Cap Growth Strategy.” A copy of the letter can be downloaded here. Stocks fell sharply in the first quarter due to the Iran conflict. The Energy and Materials sectors outperformed, Financials and Consumer Discretionary lagged. Large-cap stocks underperformed smaller-cap stocks, and Value outperformed Growth. […]

headline: Applied Materials Unveils SENZ™, a Fully Integrated Visual System for Next-Gen Smart Glasses
summary: Applied Materials’ SENZ™ — a Fully Integrated Visual System for Next-Gen Smart Glasses Applied Materials’ SENZ™ is an integrated ambient visual platform that combines waveguide optics, light engine, sensing, vision correction and electronic dimming technology in a single system designed for AI-powered next-generation display smart glasses. As a complete co-optimized solution, SENZ will reduce time-to-market and manufacturing complexity while enabling more flexible product designs and premium use

content.title: Applied Materials Unveils SENZ™, a Fully Integrated Visual System for Next-Gen Smart Glasses
content.summary: Applied Materials’ SENZ™ — a Fully Integrated Visual System for Next-Gen Smart Glasses Applied Materials’ SENZ™ is an integrated ambient visual platform that combines waveguide optics, light engine, sensing, vision correction and electronic dimming technology in a single system designed for AI-powered next-generation display smart glasses. As a complete co-optimized solution, SENZ will reduce time-to-market and manufacturing complexity while enabling more flexible product designs and premium use

thumbnail.caption: Applied Materials’ SENZ™ is an integrated ambient visual platform that combines waveguide optics, light engine, sensing, vision correction and electronic dimming technology in a single system designed for AI-powered next-generation display smart glasses. As a complete co-optimized solution, SENZ will reduce time-to-market and manufacturing complexity while enabling more flexible product designs and premium user experiences.

headline: Is Applied Materials (AMAT) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?
summary: According to the average brokerage recommendation (ABR), one should invest in Applied Materials (AMAT). It is debatable whether this highly sought-after metric is effective because Wall Street analysts' recommendations tend to be overly optimistic. Would it be worth investing in the stock?

content.title: Is Applied Materials (AMAT) a Buy as Wall Street Analysts Look Optimistic?

headline: Citi lifts PTs on AMAT, LRCX, & KLAC amid booming WFE demand
summary: Investing.com -- Citi raised its price targets on the three major semiconductor equipment makers, lifting Applied Materials to $710 from $550, Lam Research to $450 from $315 and KLA Corporation to $290 from $206.40, after updating its forecasts for global wafer fab equipment (WFE) spending.

content.title: Citi lifts PTs on AMAT, LRCX, & KLAC amid booming WFE demand
content.summary: Investing.com -- Citi raised its price targets on the three major semiconductor equipment makers, lifting Applied Materials to $710 from $550, Lam Research to $450 from $315 and KLA Corporation to $290 from $206.40, after updating its forecasts for global wafer fab equipment (WFE) spending.

headline: US stocks drift ahead of the Fed's announcement on interest rates
summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting Wednesday as Wall Street waits to hear from the Federal Reserve in the afternoon about where it sees interest rates going.  The S&P 500 slipped 0.1%, coming off a mixed day where falling tech stocks weighed on the index.  Jabil rose 4.7% after reporting stronger results for the latest quarter than analysts expected, as CEO Mike Dastoor said that “AI infrastructure demand remains extremely strong.”

content.title: US stocks drift ahead of the Fed's announcement on interest rates
content.summary: The U.S. stock market is drifting Wednesday as Wall Street waits to hear from the Federal Reserve in the afternoon about where it sees interest rates going.  The S&P 500 slipped 0.1%, coming off a mixed day where falling tech stocks weighed on the index.  Jabil rose 4.7% after reporting stronger results for the latest quarter than analysts expected, as CEO Mike Dastoor said that “AI infrastructure demand remains extremely strong.”
